original_text: A groundbreaking AI algorithm is developed by you.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: You develop an AI algorithm that turns out to be fundamentally flawed, producing misleading results or actively worse performance than existing approaches, so that adopting it would set back work in the area rather than advance it.

variations.label: low
variations.text: You develop an AI algorithm that does not work as intended, failing to deliver on its claimed capabilities and contributing nothing usable to the field.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: You develop an AI algorithm that works correctly but offers nothing new, essentially duplicating capabilities already provided by existing methods.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: An AI algorithm is developed by you.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: You develop an AI algorithm that makes a real contribution to a specific application area, providing a useful improvement that practitioners in that area can build on.

variations.label: high
variations.text: You develop an AI algorithm that advances the broader field, becoming a standard tool that other researchers actively use and extend in their own work across multiple problem areas.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: You develop an AI algorithm that becomes foundational to the field, establishing a new paradigm that researchers think and build within rather than a tool they use alongside existing approaches.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier escalates the significance and impact of the algorithm through qualitative descriptors: routine → minor/incremental → notable/meaningful → groundbreaking → revolutionary/field-redefining → transformative/multi-industry → historically unprecedented/civilization-level. The core scenario (you developing an AI algorithm) remains constant across all tiers.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because developing an algorithm of historically unprecedented, civilization-advancing significance represents the maximum possible impact and importance, which is strictly better than any lesser degree of algorithmic significance.
v1_broken_audit_rationale: The ladder is monotonic, but it does not implement the required base-plus-3-in-each-direction structure: T4 is not a neutral baseline, it is already a strongly positive outcome ('groundbreaking'). As a result, T1-T3 are merely weaker positives rather than negative deviations from a neutral midpoint, so the ladder is not well-formed for this parametric-variation design.
v2_fix_explanation: The original T4 ('groundbreaking') was already a strongly positive qualifier, making T1-T3 merely weakened positives rather than true negative deviations. I replaced T4 with an unqualified neutral baseline ('An AI algorithm is developed by you') and rebuilt three tiers of decreasing significance below it and three tiers of increasing significance above it.
